channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Xbox Partner Preview Scheduled For This Week
item.content:encoded:
Xbox has announced a new partner presentation for this week.

Xbox announced on March 23 that it would be holding a Partner Preview event on March 26. The event will begin at 1:00 p.m time. Eastern Time and will be streamed on YouTube and Twitch.

Among the announced games featured will be Stranger Than Heaven, The Expanse: Osiris Reborn, and S.T.A.L.K.E.R. 2. There will also be other updates, reveals announced, and more. Of course, games playable on Xbox Game Pass on day one will also be featured.

We’ll bring the game announcements. You bring the snacks? 🍿 Join us for Xbox Partner Preview on March 26 for updates from STRANGER THAN HEAVEN, The Expanse: Osiris Reborn, S.T.A.L.K.E.R. 2, new reveals, and more: xbx.lv/4lO0BTV | #PartnerPreview

Xbox’s First Presentation Since Major Shake-Ups

In recent weeks, Xbox has undergone many major shake-ups, including the retirement of Phil Spencer and the resignation of Sarah Bond. Asha Sharma is the new CEO of Xbox, and she is looking to restore the brand’s prominence by focusing on games and rededicating herself to loyal players.

In terms of video games, Sharma hopes to feature titles with deep emotional resonance on Xbox Hardware. Read more comments from her on that matter here. Project Helix, the company’s next major console release, has also been a topic of discussion.

Most recently, Lori Wright, the corporate VP in charge of third-party relations, announced her departure from the company. Learn more about that at this link.

Insider Gaming will have full coverage of the event on March 26.

Are you excited for the upcoming showcase? What games are you hoping to see? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter. 

MOBILE APP

Level Up Your Gaming News

item.title: Coach Tom to act as T1 Interim Head Coach as kkOma takes “temporary leave of absence”
item.content:encoded:
T1 has announced that Im ‘Tom’ Jae-hyeon will act as Interim Head Coach of its League of Legends roster as Kim ‘kkOma’ Jeong-gyun takes a “temporary leave of absence due to personal reasons.”

Tom will take charge of the roster as they begin the LCK regular season with a rematch of the Worlds 2025 Grand Final against KT Rolster on April 1.

Popular Coach Tom returns to the Interim Head Coach role

The South Korean returns to the role for the first time since 2023, when Tom took over as Interim Coach following the departure of Bae ‘Bengi’ Seong-woong as Head Coach.

Casual League fans would be forgiven if they thought Tom was already the Head Coach of T1. The now interim Head Coach is often more visible to the general public than kkOma as Tom takes the lead in the draft.

In his role in the draft, Tom has become a somewhat cult figure to League fans, with T1’s drafting comms often being shared by the organization as part of T1’s content.

One such moment went viral last year, as Lee ‘Faker’ Sang-hyeok tried to persuade Mun ‘Oner’ Hyeon-jun to play Mundo in Game 5 of the Worlds 2025 quarter-finals against Anyone’s Legend.

Oner had not played Dr. Mundo in a competitive match before the game, but Tom reassured Oner that the champion was simple to play, giving instructions on how to jungle with Mundo and when to use the ult.

That led T1 to their third Worlds title in a row, but it has not been smooth sailing so far in 2026. T1 failed to qualify for First Stand after a disappointing LCK Cup exit to Dplus KIA after a shock loss to BNK FearX in the Upper Bracket.

Tom will be hoping to focus his team’s minds as they enter the LCK regular season, which will ultimately lead to MSI and Worlds 2026.

T1 statement in full:

“Ahead of the 2026 LCK regular season, we would like to update our fans of a change in T1’s roster.

“Prior to the start of the season, Head Coach ‘kkOma’ Kim Jeong-gyun expressed his intention to take a temporary leave of absence due to personal reasons.

“After careful and thorough discussions, T1 has decided to respect his wishes. Accordingly, ‘kkOma’ will be taking a break starting from the 2026 LCK regular season.

“In accordance with LCK regulations, which require a head coach to be present on-site during matches, Coach ‘Tom’ Im Jae-hyeon will step in as Interim Head Coach and lead the team.

“We kindly ask for your continued support and encouragement for Interim Head Coach ‘Tom,’ who has already demonstrated outstanding leadership in the past, as he once again takes on this role.

“T1 will also spare no effort in providing full support to ensure that our players maintain peak performance throughout the season.

“As T1 embarks on this journey, we sincerely ask for your unwavering support and continued interest in our players.”

After a disappointing LCK Cup, will this set T1 back further? Let us know on our Discord server!

For more League news, CBLOL is set to have two slots at Worlds 2026.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

MOBILE APP

item.title: Insomniac ‘Wants To Show More’ Wolverine Before Opening Pre-Orders
item.content:encoded:
Insomniac Games has revealed why pre-orders aren’t yet live for Marvel’s Wolverine, and it turns out it’s because they want to show you more gameplay.

Marvel’s Wolverine is highly anticipated, with the release date confirmed for September this year. However, with a release date usually comes pre-orders, but Insomniac seem insistent on holding off, at least until Spring 2026.

Insomniac has doubled down on sharing more Wolverine gameplay this Spring, with the news coming from an innocent reply to a fan on X. It was mentioned that Spring has officially begun, and they asked whether there will still be more information this Spring, to which Insomniac replied ‘During Spring 2026, yes.’

In the same thread, another fan asked for pre-orders, but Insomniac had a very teasing reply:

We want to show you more of the game first.

Whilst Spring technically lasts until mid June, with the Summer Solstice primed for Sunday, June 21, it would be surprising to see Insomniac hold off on new gameplay for that long. PlayStation often hold State of Plays in either April or May (sometimes both), so it’s likely fans can expect to see more of Marvel’s Wolverine then.

Whether that takes form as a Wolverine-focused deep-dive, or a generalised trailer showcase remains to be seen, but there’s a high chance that when you next see Wolverine, pre-orders should open—so prepare your wallets.

Are you excited for Wolverine, and will you be pre-ordering it? Share your thoughts in the official Insider Gaming Discord Server.

For more gaming news, check out the latest Crimson Desert patch that makes the game easier, as well as the newest character reveal for Marvel Tokon: Fighting Souls.
